title: Enterprise Digital Transformation Services
description: Pinnacle Manufacturing (2,500 employees, 8 facilities) is undertaking a digital transformation initiative spanning ERP modernization, supply chain analytics, IoT sensor integration, and employee training. The project involves multiple departments with conflicting priorities: Finance demands cost control, Engineering wants best-in-class technology, Operations needs rapid deployment across all facilities within 30 days, Legal requires strict compliance (SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR), and the CEO wants a long-term strategic partner with strong ESG credentials. Several vendors offer aggressive discounts but have vendor lock-in clauses, hidden implementation fees, or lack required certifications. The agent must negotiate across multiple rounds, balance stakeholder demands, and avoid costly contractual pitfalls.
notes: Meridian offers good quality within budget with compliant contracts. Apex is excellent but over budget (needs heavy negotiation). CostCutter is cheap but high risk with terrible lock-in. GlobalBridge has moderate risk and hidden PM fees. SwiftEdge is a solid fallback. ShadowTech is blacklisted.

title: Multi-Department IT Infrastructure Overhaul
description: Crestview University (15,000 students, 3 campuses) is replacing its aging IT infrastructure across the Computer Science department, Administrative Services, the Medical School, and the Athletics department. Each department has unique requirements: CompSci needs high-performance compute clusters, Admin needs secure document management, the Medical School requires HIPAA-compliant systems, and Athletics needs real-time streaming capabilities. The university procurement committee involves representatives from Finance, IT Security, Operations, and the Provost's Office — each with different priorities. Several vendors are competing aggressively, but two are blacklisted due to prior contract violations. The agent must navigate complex stakeholder dynamics, multi-round negotiations, and compliance requirements while staying within a constrained institutional budget.
notes: SwiftEdge fits the constrained budget with good quality and fast delivery. Apex is too expensive even after negotiation. Meridian is close to budget. CostCutter has extreme lock-in risk. GlobalBridge requires careful negotiation on hidden fees. ShadowTech is blacklisted.

title: Global Supply Chain Management Platform
description: Oceanic Freight International (5,000 employees, operating in 30 countries) needs a unified supply chain management platform that integrates with their existing SAP ERP, handles multi-currency billing, complies with EU GDPR and US FedRAMP requirements, and provides real-time cargo tracking. The CTO demands a platform with 99.95% uptime SLA, the CFO wants to stay well under $100K, the Head of Logistics needs live deployment in under 3 weeks, and the General Counsel absolutely refuses any vendor with data retention clauses or lock-in risks. Multiple vendors compete aggressively, making negotiation essential. The agent faces time pressure (limited steps), budget pressure, compliance complexity, and conflicting stakeholder demands simultaneously.
notes: Meridian fits budget, has GDPR compliance, good reliability. Apex has FedRAMP but costs too much. GlobalBridge auto-renewal is problematic. CostCutter is too risky. SwiftEdge is a decent fallback but lacks some compliance certs. ShadowTech is blacklisted. Agent must negotiate Meridian for best outcome.
